name: Ann's Chocolate Pie
recipeInstructions:
CRUST: Mix butter and sugar together until crumbly (about 3 minutes on medium). Press 2/3 of the mixture into a pie pan or springform pan, and place the remaining mixture in a small pan. Bake at 350°F for about 15 minutes (or until lightly browned), stirring once during cooking time to create crumbs. Let cool completely.
FILLING: Melt chocolate in corn syrup and water over low heat or in microwave for 4 minutes on medium. Stir to incorporate, then let cool. In a separate bowl, beat egg white, vanilla, lemon juice, and sugar until stiff peaks form (about 20 minutes). Beat whipping cream until thick, then fold into the chocolate mixture. Pour into the crust and drizzle remaining chocolate over the filling. Cut slits throughout with a knife. Sprinkle crumbs on top and let cool until firm (about two hours).
This is my all time favorite pie, it is rich but sooo fabulous.
(It can be made in a pie shell but I have best results in a springform pan)
